memory.summary: I noticed the Tuesday block shifted—language and workout are still happening, but the lounge outlets being all occupied could slow me down. Also, my friend wants to order delivery because the weather’s nice, which might derail my routine.

Just now my friend messaged “Where are you? We ordered first,” so they went ahead without me. That updates things: I’m probably already behind their timeline, and I’ll need to catch up without letting it hijack my energy and routine.

My focus curve is dropping just as the workout window is approaching, and with 2 hours of sleep debt my stress feels like it’s barely under control. I’m behind my friend’s delivery timeline, so I need to catch up carefully.

I didn’t get new info, but I’m still running on 2 hours of sleep debt, so my energy is low and my stress is hovering. Since delivery already happened, I’m behind and need to recover without letting it derail my routine.

With no new info, I’m still stuck with the same reality: I’m running on 2 hours of sleep debt, energy is low, and stress is barely contained. The update is internal—I need catch-up work that doesn’t blow up my routine.
No new facts changed, but I’m still operating on 2 hours of sleep debt with low energy; stress is only barely contained. It feels like catch-up is the same task, just with worse internal bandwidth.

Nothing new happened, but my internal bandwidth is still the problem: I’m running on 2 hours of sleep debt, energy is low, and stress is barely contained—so the “catch-up” feels heavier than it should.
Nothing new actually changed, but I’m still running on 2 hours of sleep debt with energy this low; stress feels only “barely contained,” so even familiar catch-up tasks hit harder than they should.

Nothing new changed, but with my energy at ~1.2 and 2 hours of sleep debt, I’m noticing stress landing harder than before. Catch-up still feels heavier—like my bandwidth is shrinking, not the workload.

Nothing new happened, but with my energy at ~0.9 and 2 hours of sleep debt, I feel my bandwidth tightening again. Stress sits lower than before, yet it’s still enough to make catch-up tasks feel disproportionately heavy.

desires: Avoid falling behind in a way that makes me look unreliable (especially to my close cohort), even if I have to do it imperfectly.
Get through the “catch-up” feeling without it costing me sleep again tomorrow—stopping the sleep-debt spiral is a bigger need than finishing everything.
Protect my remaining focus by choosing the smallest doable next step (like language for momentum) instead of risking a bigger task that will just tank my energy.
Keep stress from spiking by staying in control through routines (meal/workout/language) rather than letting it turn into anxious overthinking.

goals.description: Finish language study: keep doing the next smallest chunk right now until the remaining ~90 minutes are done by 14:30. If focus slips, immediately resume with the next tiny step—no detours, no “just one extra thing.”
goals.rationale: This protects my sleep and focus by forcing momentum on the one soft academic win, and it helps me avoid feeling unreliable.

goals.description: Send the friend chat boundary in one message only (no extra context): “날씨 좋아! I’ll handle my plan first—can eat/order something simple after language (or after 14:30).”
goals.rationale: Direct, low-drama compromise that reduces guilt/unreliable vibes with minimal energy cost—exactly my conflict style.

goals.description: Immediately after language ends: do the light workout for 30 minutes and be finished by ~15:30. Movement only; nothing else attached.
goals.rationale: Routine-controlled stress drop without heavy focus—keeps me from spiraling while preserving energy for later.

goals.description: After workout: start lunch right away and be eating by 16:00 (order if needed). Don’t delay by sitting there deciding.
goals.rationale: Feeding myself on time blocks the overthinking→procrastination loop and reduces tomorrow’s punishment.

goals.description: Start Language study immediately and finish the full 90 minutes by 14:30 (no “just order delivery” detours). If my friend pushes delivery, I’ll suggest a quick plan: eat after I’m done or do a half-step like grabbing something simple, then return to study.
goals.rationale: My biggest fear is getting derailed and feeling like I let people down. This protects my academic priority and keeps me reliable with my cohort.

goals.description: Handle the friend chat with a quick, calm boundary: acknowledge (“날씨 좋아!”) + offer an alternative that keeps my schedule (food after language / or order simple after 14:30).
goals.rationale: I’m compromising by keeping the relationship smooth, but still defending my time so I don’t spiral from guilt or stress.

goals.description: Do the Light workout right after language (about 30 minutes) and don’t negotiate it down—aim to be done by ~15:30.
goals.rationale: I want tomorrow’s energy intact. If I keep the workout rhythm, meals and focus stop slipping into catch-up mode.

goals.description: Plan lunch for after the workout and actually eat (basic, easy meal) by 16:00—choose the least logistically annoying option so I don’t postpone meals.
goals.rationale: Basic meals are part of maintaining momentum; missing that rhythm is how stress stacks and sleep debt gets worse.

desires: Finish language on time without getting derailed by my friend’s “let’s just order delivery” plan—so I don’t feel like I let everyone down.
Don’t tank my energy tomorrow: I want to avoid stacking stress and ending this day sleepier than I already am (2 hours debt is not nothing).
Keep workouts and basic meals from getting awkwardly postponed—if I miss the rhythm, everything starts sliding and then I’ll spiral into catching up later.
Avoid looking unreliable (or overly stressed) to my cohort—especially since they’re super close and I don’t want them to think I’m drifting.

trajectory.observation: Worked on Language study (90 min) for 1.5h; progress=0.13.

goals.description: Reply to friend chat (text) with a warm acknowledgment: “날씨 좋아!” + a clear alternative that protects my timeline: I’ll handle my plan first and can eat/order something simple after language (or after 14:30). No debate—just one concrete option and a time.
goals.rationale: I want to stay reliable and not look flaky, while still preventing the conversation from hijacking my schedule and spiking stress.

goals.description: Lock in language study right now: finish the full 90 minutes by 14:30. If anyone pushes delivery/changes plans, I repeat the same boundary: I’ll eat after language or choose a simple option after 14:30—then return immediately to studying (no side detours).
goals.rationale: This protects my academic priority and my self-image as dependable, which is the main thing I’m anxious about.

goals.description: Pick and commit to an easy, low-friction lunch after the workout and actually eat by 16:00 (no postponing).
goals.rationale: Eating on time keeps stress from stacking and helps my focus stay steady instead of turning into catch-up chaos.

desires: Don’t look like I’m letting my friends down—especially since they already ordered without me and I hate being the flaky one.
Finish language study fast enough to feel caught up, without letting the delivery/social slip derail my routine and push my stress higher.
Protect tomorrow’s energy despite the sleep debt—so I don’t trade today’s catch-up for feeling wrecked later.
Keep the compromise vibe: stay engaged with friends, but not so much that they hijack my plan or force me into last-minute decisions.
